issues: Draft headline 'All-Star Hockey Game Results' is fabricated — no clear headline is present in the OCR text.
Draft says Coastal led 5-3 after two periods; OCR says the leads were 4-0 and 5-3 (period leads), not specifically 5-3 after two periods — the framing is misleading.

Draft says Interior 'rallied to outscore the Coastal all-stars despite trailing 5-3 after two periods' implying a comeback win, but the OCR does not state the final score or confirm an Interior win — this is an unsupported inference.

Draft says Carignan 'shutting out the Coastal team in the final 30 minutes' — OCR says 'final 30 minutes', which the draft repeats correctly, but the broader narrative about an Interior comeback/win is not confirmed by the OCR.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes' Tony Bobbitt scored for the Coastal all-stars in a BCJHL all-star game against an Interior squad in Merritt, with Kelowna Packers goaltender Lucien Carignan named Interior MVP after shutting out Coastal in the final 30 minutes.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: A BCJHL all-star game pitting Coastal and Interior teams saw Richmond Sockeyes forward Tony Bobbitt score for the Coastal squad alongside goals from Darren Biggs and Mike Knapton of Nanaimo Clippers and Aaron Nosky of Delta Flyers. Interior scorers included Pat Mangold and Craig Bedard of Kelowna Packers and Darryl Kirkpatrick and Steve Manuel of host Merritt Warriors. The Coastal all-stars held leads of 4-0 and 5-3 at period breaks but were outshot 42-32, and Kelowna Packers goaltender Lucien Carignan was named Interior MVP after blanking the Coastal side in the game's final 30 minutes. The OCR page also contains an unrelated notice that the Richmond Sockeyes were hosting Abbotsford with a 7:30 p.m. game time at Richmond Arena.
review.notes: No headline is discernible in the OCR for the all-star game article — the draft headline appears to be AI-fabricated and should be left blank. The draft's suggestion of an Interior comeback win is an unsupported inference; the OCR does not state the final score. The page also contains a brief separate notice about a Sockeyes home game vs. Abbotsford, which may warrant its own scan entry.
